annotations.label: Gibbs phenomenon

anchor.snippet: the Gibbs phenomenon is the oscillatory behavior of the Fourier series of a piecewise continuously differentiable periodic function around a jump discontinuity

annotations.note: Grep for 'Gibbs' in Mathlib returns only Kullback–Leibler/CondVar hits; the Gibbs phenomenon is not defined.

annotations.label: 9% overshoot around jump

anchor.snippet: the Fourier series shows the first overshoot in the oscillatory behavior around the jump point approaching ~ 9% of the (full) jump

annotations.note: No quantitative overshoot/Wilbraham–Gibbs constant statement is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fourier series equals midpoint at jump

anchor.snippet: the Fourier series gives the average of the function's both side limits toward the point

annotations.note: Mathlib's Fourier theory in Analysis/Fourier/AddCircle has only L²/summable convergence, no Dirichlet-style pointwise midpoint result at jumps.

annotations.label: Square wave Gibbs phenomenon

anchor.snippet: The three pictures on the right demonstrate the Gibbs phenomenon for a square wave

annotations.note: Grep for 'square wave' returns no Mathlib hits and there is no square-wave Fourier series example.

annotations.label: Overshoot at general jump discontinuity

anchor.snippet: at any discontinuity of a piecewise continuously differentiable function with a jump of

annotations.note: No general-jump overshoot bound is formalized; Mathlib lacks the Gibbs phenomenon entirely.

annotations.label: Wilbraham–Gibbs constant

anchor.snippet: is sometimes known as the Wilbraham –Gibbs constant

annotations.note: Grep for 'Wilbraham' in Mathlib returns no hits.

annotations.label: Pointwise but not uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: The Gibbs phenomenon thus exhibits pointwise convergence , but not uniform convergence

annotations.note: Generic pointwise/uniform convergence notions exist, but no statement about the Fourier series of a jump function being pointwise-but-not-uniformly convergent is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convergence to midpoint by Dirichlet's theorem

anchor.snippet: the infinite sum will converge to the jump discontinuity's midpoint

annotations.note: Dirichlet's pointwise convergence theorem for Fourier series is absent from Mathlib (only L²/summable variants in Analysis/Fourier/AddCircle).

annotations.label: Smoothness controls Fourier coefficient decay

anchor.snippet: the smoothness of a function controls the decay rate of its Fourier coefficients

annotations.note: The Riemann–Lebesgue lemma gives qualitative decay-to-zero for L¹ functions, but no quantitative `C^k ⇒ |aₙ| = O(n^{-k})` Fourier-coefficient decay statement is formalized.

annotations.label: Absolutely convergent Fourier coefficients impossible for discontinuous function

anchor.snippet: it is impossible for a discontinuous function to have absolutely convergent Fourier coefficients

annotations.note: Mathlib shows that summable Fourier coefficients give a uniformly-convergent (hence continuous) sum in the C(AddCircle) topology, from which the contrapositive follows, but the contrapositive itself is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Elimination via non-negative kernels (Fejér)

anchor.snippet: it may be eliminated by using kernels that are never negative, such as the Fejér kernel

annotations.note: Case-insensitive grep for 'Fejér'/'fejer' returns no Mathlib hits; the Fejér kernel is not defined.

annotations.label: Formal description of the Gibbs phenomenon

anchor.section: Formal mathematical description of the Gibbs phenomenon
anchor.snippet: be a piecewise continuously differentiable function which is periodic with some period

annotations.note: The precise Gibbs limit theorem (overshoot of the partial-sum sequence at a jump) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gibbs phenomenon at every discontinuity (general case)

anchor.section: Proof of the Gibbs phenomenon in a general case
anchor.snippet: This proves how the Gibbs phenomenon occurs at every discontinuity

annotations.note: No statement about Gibbs overshoot at arbitrary jump discontinuities exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square wave partial Fourier series analysis

anchor.snippet: Next, we find the first maximum of the oscillation around the discontinuity

annotations.note: No worked square-wave partial-sum / first-maximum analysis is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gibbs phenomenon as step response of ideal low-pass filter

anchor.snippet: the Gibbs phenomenon is the step response of a low-pass filter

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of brick-wall low-pass filters or their step response; sinc/Heaviside convolution interpretation of Gibbs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Overshoot magnitude as sine integral of sinc tail

anchor.snippet: the magnitude of the undershoot is thus exactly the integral of the left tail until the first negative zero

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the sine integral Si and the identification of the Gibbs overshoot with a sinc tail integral.

annotations.label: Partial sum at first maximum converges to Si(π)/π

anchor.snippet: the expression in the square brackets is a Riemann sum approximation to the integral

annotations.note: The midpoint Riemann sum → ∫₀^π sinc identification giving the Wilbraham–Gibbs limit is not formalized; Mathlib has no sinc-integral evaluation of this form.
